name: Low-Fat Chocolate Raspberry Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 24 Plain chocolate wafer cookies (half of a 9 ounce box)
3 tablespoons sugar-free seedless black raspberry preserves
recipeInstructions:
For Fruit Topping:
1 (10 ounce) box sweetened frozen raspberries, thawed
2 tablespoons cornstarch
1. In a food processor or blender, grind the plain chocolate wafer cookies into fine crumbs.
2. Generously coat an 8.1/2 inch or 9 inch springform pan with nonstick cooking spray. Pour the crumbs into the pan and tilt to coat the bottom and sides evenly. Set aside.
3. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
4. With an electric mixer, beat the fat-free cream cheese until soft. Add the sugar, cocoa, vanilla extract, and raspberry preserves, and continue beating until all ingredients are incorporated.
5. Add the egg substitutes, fat-free sour cream, and salt, and continue beating until no lumps remain and the mixture is smooth and liquid. You may need to beat for several minutes to achieve this consistency.
6. Pour the batter into the prepared pan. Place in the oven and immediately reduce the temperature to 300°F (150°C).
7. Bake for one hour, or until the center is set and firm to the touch.
8. Turn off the oven and allow the cheesecake to remain in the cooling oven for one hour longer. Remove and cool completely.
9. Refrigerate overnight before serving.
10. To make the raspberry sauce, puree the raspberries in a food processor or blender. Strain the pulp through a tea strainer, pressing to remove all seeds. Discard the seeds.
11. Place the cornstarch in a saucepan and stir in the raspberry juice. Bring to a boil, stirring constantly until thickened. Remove from heat immediately to prevent lumps from forming. Cool and chill.
12. Serve one tablespoon of sauce on top of each slice of cheesecake.
Makes 10 servings
Per serving, including raspberry sauce:
Calories: 254
Fat: 3.7 grams
Cholesterol: 11.3 milligrams
Sodium: 405 milligrams
